title: test: add node backward-compat regression coverage
description: Adds Playwright-based browser compatibility testing and backward compatibility tests for the Node.js API, including a new browser compatibility fixture and improvements to the test setup.
body: This pull request introduces Playwright-based browser compatibility testing and adds a new browser compatibility fixture, alongside improvements to the test setup and backward compatibility tests for the Node.js API. The main changes are grouped below: **Browser compatibility testing infrastructure:** * Added Playwright as a dev dependency and introduced a new `test:playwright` script in `package.json` to enable browser-based testing. * Added a Playwright configuration file (`playwright.config.ts`) to specify test directory, matching pattern, browser settings, and web server startup for browser tests. **Browser compatibility fixture:** * Added a new browser compatibility test page (`browser-compat.page.tsx`) that checks for absence of Node globals, correct rendering of PNG buffers, and error handling for filesystem paths in the browser. * Added a Playwright test (`browser-compat.pw.ts`) to automate and validate the browser compatibility fixture, ensuring correct behavior in a browser environment. **Node.js API backward compatibility tests:** * Added new Bun-based tests (`node-api.test.ts`) to verify that the Node.js API still supports filesystem paths and returns Node Buffers as expected.

title: repro: through_pad routes producing NaN KiCad PCB segments
description: Adds a repro using alarmv2.json showing that valid through_pad route points are converted into KiCad PCB segments with NaN coordinates, causing kicadts parsing to fail. core emits a valid circuit-json through_pad route point with startend coordinates, matching the circuit-json schema. where the converter assumes every route point has top-level xy, producing NaN for valid through_pad points.
body:
Adds a repro using alarmv2.json showing that valid through_pad route points are converted into KiCad PCB segments with NaN coordinates, causing kicadts parsing to fail.

core emits a valid circuit-json through_pad route point with start/end coordinates, matching the circuit-json schema.

where the converter assumes every route point has top-level x/y, producing NaN for valid through_pad points.

title: fix: ensure deterministic unnamed subcircuit connectivity keys
description: Ensures deterministic generation of unnamed subcircuit connectivity map keys by using a combination of subcircuit_id, source_group_id, and render ID, reducing key collisions and inconsistencies across renders.
body:
This pull request improves the determinism and correctness of how unnamed subcircuit connectivity map keys are generated for groups, traces, ports, and nets. The logic now consistently uses a combination of `subcircuit_id`, `source_group_id`, or a fallback render ID to ensure that keys are stable and unique across renders. Existing test fixtures and outputs are updated to match the new key format, and a new test verifies that the generated keys remain deterministic.

Key changes include:

**Connectivity Map Key Generation Improvements:**

- Updated the logic in `Group_doInitialSourceAddConnectivityMapKey` to generate `subcircuit_connectivity_map_key` using `subcircuitName ?? unnamedsubcircuit${group.subcircuit_id ?? group.source_group_id ?? group._renderId}` instead of relying solely on `_renderId`. This ensures keys are more stable and meaningful, especially for unnamed subcircuits. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-a33d22a38d5e4a6a8d63348efa30f618119ba1690c75e4d6c8f3506c9683efc0L49-R49) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-a33d22a38d5e4a6a8d63348efa30f618119ba1690c75e4d6c8f3506c9683efc0L71-R71) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-a33d22a38d5e4a6a8d63348efa30f618119ba1690c75e4d6c8f3506c9683efc0L99-R99)

**Testing and Validation:**

- Added a new test (`group-unnamed-subcircuit-connectivity-map-key.test.tsx`) that renders circuits with unnamed subcircuits multiple times and asserts that the generated connectivity map keys are deterministic and consistent across renders.

**Fixture and Test Data Updates:**

- Updated all relevant test fixtures and JSON assets (such as `simple-circuit.json`) to use the new, deterministic `subcircuit_connectivity_map_key` format, replacing previous keys that used only the render ID. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-18c79889f6ca2769912318cd8a1cba5d004402fb06e298fd19a3112518a3b741L31-R31) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-18c79889f6ca2769912318cd8a1cba5d004402fb06e298fd19a3112518a3b741L51-R51) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-18c79889f6ca2769912318cd8a1cba5d004402fb06e298fd19a3112518a3b741L84-R84) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-18c79889f6ca2769912318cd8a1cba5d004402fb06e298fd19a3112518a3b741L192-R192)

These changes make subcircuit connectivity mapping more robust and predictable, reducing the likelihood of key collisions or inconsistencies between renders.

title: Gate recent command palette package fetches on dialog open
description: Prevents the command palette from fetching recent packages while it is closed, ensuring background requests only occur when the palette is actively used.
body:
### Summary
This change prevents the command palette from fetching recent packages while it is closed.

### What changed
- Updated the `recentPackages` query in `src/components/CmdKMenu.tsx`
- Added `open &&` to the React Query `enabled` condition

### Why
Previously, the query could run as soon as `CmdKMenu` mounted, as long as the user was logged in and the search input was empty. That meant background network work was happening even if the command palette was never opened.

### Behavior before
- `CmdKMenu` mounted
- Logged-in user with empty search state triggered the recent packages query
- Request could run while the palette was closed

### Behavior after
- `CmdKMenu` mounts without fetching recent packages
- The query only becomes eligible when the command palette is open
- Recent packages are fetched only when the user actually uses the palette

### Impact
- Reduces unnecessary background requests
- Avoids loading palette-only data until it is needed
- Keeps command palette data fetching aligned with user interaction

### Testing
- Verified the query `enabled` condition now requires `open`

title: Document QFN thermal pad footprint strings
description: This PR updates the documentation for the QFN package footprint to clarify and expand support for thermal pads, as well as to improve parameter descriptions and preview examples.
body: This pull request updates the documentation for the QFN (Quad Flat No-Lead) package footprint to clarify and expand support for thermal pads, as well as to improve parameter descriptions and preview examples. QFN footprint documentation improvements: * Added documentation for the new `thermalpad` parameter, allowing users to add a centered exposed pad to the QFN footprint and optionally specify its dimensions. * Updated the `FootprintPreview` examples to include QFN variants with thermal pads and custom pad sizes. * Revised parameter descriptions: replaced `body_size` with separate `w` (width) and `h` (height) parameters, updated the default for `pl` (pin length), and clarified the meaning of each parameter.

body: Adds QFP footprinter documentation for exposed thermal pads, including both default `qfp32_thermalpad` and sized `qfp32_thermalpad3x3mm` examples. Also updates the QFP parameter table to match `tscircuit/props`, replacing the stale `body_size` entry with `w` and `h`, and documenting `legsoutside`. ## Validation - Confirmed QFP examples parse with `@tscircuit/footprinter` - Ran `bun run typecheck` - Ran `bun run build`

title: Add index.tsx export barrel for all SparkFun boards
description: Adds index.tsx export barrels to every SparkFun board folder so all 96 boards match the repo convention and can be imported as modules, while fixing broken filenames and refreshing snapshots for renamed circuit files.
body:
# Add `index.tsx` export barrels for all SparkFun boards

## Summary

Adds `index.tsx` export barrels to every SparkFun board folder so all **96** boards match the repo convention and can be imported as modules. Also fixes a few broken filenames and refreshes snapshots for boards whose circuit files were renamed.

## Changes

- **49× `index.tsx`** — thin re-exports of each board’s `*.circuit.tsx` default export (same pattern as existing boards like HMC6343 / microB USB)
- **SparkFun-SD-Card-Breakout** — rename `inxed.tsx` → `index.tsx`
- **SparkFun-Electret-Microphone-Breakout** — rename `*.circuiit.tsx` → `*.circuit.tsx`, add `index.tsx`, update snapshots
- **SparkFun-Opto-isolator-Breakout** — rename `*.tsx` → `*.circuit.tsx`, add `index.tsx`, update snapshots

No changes to board schematics, layouts, or component logic — export plumbing and naming consistency only.

## Why

Per [README](./README.md), each board folder should expose:

1. **`index.tsx`** — importable public API for consumers
2. **`<name>.circuit.tsx`** — source of truth used by `tsci dev` and `tsci snapshot`

Previously **47/96** boards had `index.tsx`. The rest had working circuits and snapshots but could not be imported from the folder path without knowing the exact circuit filename (which often differs from the folder name, e.g. `(Qwiic)` in the folder vs `-Qwiic` in the file).

### Example

```tsx
// boards/SparkFun-Digital-Compass-HMC6343-(Qwiic)/index.tsx
import HMC6343 from "./SparkFun-Digital-Compass-HMC6343-Qwiic.circuit"

export default HMC6343
```

## Before / after

| Metric | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Boards with `index.tsx` | 47 | 96 |
| Boards with valid `*.circuit.tsx` | 94 | 96 |
| Filename typos (SD / Electret / Opto) | 3 | 0 |

title: Fix through_pad routes producing NaN KiCad PCB segments
description: Fixes NaN coordinates in KiCad PCB segments by reading through_pad.start and through_pad.end coordinates instead of assuming top-level xy for every route point.
body:
The converter now reads through_pad.start / through_pad.end coordinates instead of assuming every route point has top-level x / y, preventing NaN coordinates in the generated KiCad PCB.

This updates all the snapshots

title: Classify fiducial footprints as simple_fiducial
description: Classifies PCB fiducial footprints as simple_fiducial and updates the inference logic to correctly identify fiducials based on their metadata.
body:
## Summary
- classify PCB fiducial footprints as `simple_fiducial`
- pass the footprint into PCB component type inference so fiducial metadata can be used
- add a focused regression test for the Arduino Nano fiducials
- exclude `scratch/` from project typechecking so `bunx tsc --noEmit` checks the actual repo sources

## Why
PCB footprint inference was only using the reference designator. That caused fiducials to collapse to `simple_chip` even when the footprint metadata clearly identified them as fiducials.

## Validation
- `bun run format`
- `bun test tests/pcb/fiducial-component-type.test.ts tests/pcb/footprint-properties.test.ts`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`

## Notes
- Full `bun test` is still red in this repo because of existing snapshot mismatches in unrelated visual parity tests. Those failures are not introduced by this fiducial change.

title: Classify LED footprints as simple_led
description: Summary classify Arduino Nano LED footprints as simple_led instead of simple_diode pass the full footprint into PCB component type inference so diode designators can use footprint metadata add a focused regression test for LED vs diode classification  Root Cause PCB ftype inference was using only the reference designator prefix. That caused D parts with LED footprints to collapse to simple_diode even when the footprint metadata clearly identified them as LEDs.  Validation bun test testspcbled-component-type.test.ts testspcbfootprint-properties.test.ts
body:
## Summary
- classify Arduino Nano LED footprints as `simple_led` instead of `simple_diode`
- pass the full footprint into PCB component type inference so diode designators can use footprint metadata
- add a focused regression test for LED vs diode classification

## Root Cause
PCB `ftype` inference was using only the reference designator prefix. That caused `D*` parts with LED footprints to collapse to `simple_diode` even when the footprint metadata clearly identified them as LEDs.

## Validation
- `bun test tests/pcb/led-component-type.test.ts tests/pcb/footprint-properties.test.ts`

title: Add repro for dense parallel header-to-IC schematic routing
description: Adds a schematic trace solver repro covering a dense set of parallel direct connections from a 9-pin J2 header to U1, exercising routing behavior where adjacent nets must cross between closely spaced left-side pins and mixedstaggered right-side pins.
body:
## Summary

Adds `example40`, a schematic trace solver repro covering a dense set of parallel direct connections from a 9-pin `J2` header to `U1`. The case exercises routing behavior where adjacent nets must cross between closely spaced left-side pins and mixed/staggered right-side pins.

## Changes

- Added `tests/assets/example40.json`
- Added `site/examples/example40.page.tsx` for the pipeline debugger
- Added `tests/examples/example40.test.ts`
- Added the generated SVG snapshot for `example40`

## Validation

- `bun test tests/examples/example40.test.ts`
- `bun run format:check tests/assets/example40.json site/examples/example40.page.tsx tests/examples/example40.test.ts`

title: Add benchmark CI with main-vs-PR comparison tables and reusable result artifacts
description: Add a continuous integration workflow for benchmarking that includes automated instructions, persistent artifact reuse, and detailed reporting with comparison tables for pull requests.
body:
- Comment-driven /benchmark workflow execution for PRs
- Automatic benchmark instructions posted on pull request creation
- Persistent main-branch artifact reuse to avoid redundant reruns
- Structured benchmark JSON and markdown report generation
- Rich benchmark summary comments with comparison tables and sample-level metrics
- Support for dataset, solver, family, sample, and limit benchmarking controls
- Artifact upload/download integration for benchmark history
- Improved srj18 dataset discovery across repository layouts
- Benchmark result persistence and reporting outputs
- Workflow support for PR-triggered benchmark validation and manual dispatch execution

title: Introduce Dedicated Jobs Management Workspace with Routed Queue Navigation and Export Progress Tracking

title: Add KiCad archive helper utilities
description: Adds a small KiCad archive helper module for working with Ultra Librarian KiCad zip buffers, which reads archive buffers, identifies supported KiCad symbolfootprint files, rejects unsafe archive paths during extraction, and exports helper APIs from the package entrypoint.
body: ## What this does Adds a small KiCad archive helper module for working with Ultra Librarian KiCad zip buffers. - reads archive buffers and identifies `.kicad_sym` and `.kicad_mod` entries - extracts only supported KiCad symbol/footprint files - rejects unsafe archive paths during extraction - exports the helper APIs from the package entrypoint - adds Bun tests with in-memory zip fixtures ## Safety No downloaded Ultra Librarian zips or extracted generated CAD files are committed. Tests create fake in-memory archives only.

- adds a typed bridge client with unit tests

bun run typecheck
bun run build

body: Padding is already applied by `Group.calculatePcbGroupBounds()` (called via super), so no additional padding adjustment needed here.

title: Partial integration of breakout-point-solver
description: Integrates the breakout-point-solver functionality into the rendering process for PCB layout, allowing for automatic placement of breakout points based on solver output.

title: Add AutoPlacedBreakoutPoint class and implement doInitialCreateAutoplacedBreakoutPoints phase
description: Adds a new AutoPlacedBreakoutPoint class and implements functionality to automatically create breakout points for ports whose traces cross the breakout boundary.

title: Add CreateAutoplacedBreakoutPoints render phase
description: Adds a new render phase for creating autoplaced breakout points in the rendering process.

title: Skip pcb_component s entries in PNP export
description: Removes PCB components generated for manually placed vias from the PNP CSV export, ensuring only assemblable parts are included.
body: The PNP exporter was including PCB components generated for manually placed vias, which produced rows like pcb_component_6, pcb_component_7, etc. These are routing objects, not assemblable parts, so they should not appear in the PNP CSV.

title: .github/workflows: add Blacksmith testbox configuration
description: Adds Blacksmith Testbox configuration for running CI against local changes.

Blacksmith Testboxes let your agents run CI against local changes, instantly.

This PR adds `.github/workflows/benchmark-testbox.yml` and the `blacksmith-testbox` agent skill (`.claude/skills/blacksmith-testbox/SKILL.md`).

title: update test fixture to support snapshots / fix type errors
description: Updates test fixtures to support snapshot testing and resolves type errors in the codebase.

title: fix label-label collision - example39/41
description: Fixes label-label collision in schematic trace solver to prevent overlapping labels during net label placement.

title: Make GLTF-to-PNG rendering browser-safe with Node path support
description: Refactors the renderGLTFToPNGBuffer implementation to improve modularity and cross-environment support by moving the function to the main library and enhancing input handling for various GLTF sources.
body:
This pull request refactors the `renderGLTFToPNGBuffer` implementation to improve modularity and cross-environment (Node and browser) support. The main change moves the function from the CLI-specific file to a new module in the main library, and rewrites its logic to handle different GLTF input types and sources more robustly.

Key changes include:

**Refactoring and Relocation:**
- Moved `renderGLTFToPNGBuffer` from the CLI module (`../cli/renderGLTFToPNGBuffer`) to the main render library (`./render/renderGLTFToPNGBuffer`) and updated the export path in `lib/index.ts`.

**Cross-Environment and Input Handling Improvements:**
- Rewrote `renderGLTFToPNGBuffer` to:
  - Support GLTF input as JSON objects, JSON strings, URLs, and Node.js filesystem paths.
  - Automatically detect whether the code is running in Node or a browser and handle file access accordingly.
  - Add error handling for unsupported input types in browsers.

**Node.js Filesystem Support:**
- Added a helper (`loadNodeFilesystemGLTF`) that dynamically imports Node.js modules to load GLTF files and their resources (buffers, images) from the filesystem, including support for data URIs and bufferViews.

**Rendering Pipeline Integration:**
- Refactored the rendering pipeline to use `createSceneFromGLTF`, `renderDrawCalls`, and `encodePNGToBuffer`, consolidating resource handling and rendering logic for consistency.

title: Add support for pill shape smt pads
description: Adds support for pill shape SMT pads in the PCB design process, allowing for more versatile pad shapes in circuit designs.

title: Introduces end-to-end benchmark concurrency configuration
description: Adds a concurrency configuration option to the benchmark workflow, allowing users to specify the level of concurrency for benchmarking operations.

title: Match benchmark PR comparisons by dataset and cache main results for all benchmark datasets
description: Caches benchmark results for all datasets and matches PR comparisons by dataset to improve benchmarking accuracy.

title: Add the fallback-image, remove autofixable section and limit the decimal places of board size
description: Adds a fallback image for PCB previews, removes the autofixable section from the FabricatorSelector component, and limits the decimal places of board size in the PcbPreview component.

title: Add tscircuit platform parts engine example
description: Adds a real TSX tscircuit example showing how to use the TI parts engine through platform.partsEngine.
body:
## What this does

Adds a real TSX tscircuit example showing how to use the TI parts engine through `platform.partsEngine`.

- adds `createTiPlatformPartsEngine`
- makes `TiPartsEngine` compatible with the `PartsEngine` `findPart` shape
- adds a `RootCircuit` LM358 example using `PARTNER_TOKEN`
- adds a type-level check against `@tscircuit/props` `PartsEngine`

## Notes

`fetchPartCircuitJson` / automatic footprint generation for arbitrary TI parts is not implemented yet and is marked as TODO.

title: Add LM358 live bridge verification script
description: Adds a local-only verification flow for the Ultra Librarian Bridge using LM358.
body: ## What this does Adds a local-only verification flow for the Ultra Librarian Bridge using `LM358`. - adds `bun run verify:lm358-bridge` - reads `PARTNER_TOKEN` from local env only - calls the bridge search endpoint for `LM358` - downloads the KiCad v6 export zip - verifies the archive contains at least one `.kicad_sym` and one `.kicad_mod` - extracts matching KiCad files only into ignored local `imports/` output - documents how to run the check locally

title: Add repro120, the footprint should be on the bottom layer
description: Adds a test to ensure that the footprint for a component is rendered on the bottom layer of the PCB when specified.

title: Use Uint8Array-first byte helpers in shared runtime paths
description: Refactors shared byte handling to use Uint8Array-first helpers instead of direct Buffer methods in library runtime paths, while preserving existing Node Buffer return behavior.
body:
## Summary

This is PR 3 of the multi-PR browser-safety plan for public rendering entrypoints.

This PR refactors shared byte handling to use `Uint8Array`-first helpers instead of direct `Buffer.from`, `Buffer.concat`, and `Buffer.isBuffer` usage in library runtime paths, while preserving existing Node `Buffer` return behavior.

## Changes

- Added shared byte utilities for normalizing, concatenating, and decoding byte data as `Uint8Array`.
- Updated `encodePNGToBuffer` to collect PNG stream chunks as `Uint8Array` internally.
- Updated GLTF resource utilities to decode data URIs without direct `Buffer.from`.
- Kept Node compatibility by converting back to `Buffer` only at the public API boundary.
- Added focused byte helper tests and strengthened backward compatibility coverage.

## Validation

- `rg -n "Buffer\\.from|Buffer\\.concat|Buffer\\.isBuffer" lib -S`
- `bun test`
- `bun run format:check`
- `bun run build`

title: refactor: Apply two-parameter rule to PCB footprint converter functions
description: Refactors several converter functions to use a single parameter object instead of multiple positional arguments, improving code readability and maintainability without changing functional logic.
body:
This pull request refactors several converter functions and their usage to use a single parameter object instead of multiple positional arguments. This improves code readability and maintainability, making it less error-prone when passing many related parameters. No functional logic is changed; only the function signatures and their call sites are updated.

**Refactoring to parameter objects:**

* Updated `convertSilkscreenTexts`, `convertNoteTexts`, `convertNpthHoles`, and `convertCourtyardOutlines` to accept a single parameter object instead of multiple positional arguments. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-d2b50d50c7e733be9b3bf5cd86f45f7e35c58c4a24ec8bec9b4bd6e0673eccf2L6-R18) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-501a6cdc5eef208ae01dc1b8a963829eeb28f3b867943810560ea8ae0e88e55fL6-R12) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-a880b1731584386e0b959f434a5f0883ff99ae4164c473d26d31df1010c6246bL6-R12) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-cdea65ad2ed39bd2cbec0455d66c1e7d1a111f8bdbf978c8527c3250b3ff9bb3L13-R23)
* Refactored all call sites in `AddFootprintsStage` and `AddStandalonePcbElements` to use the new parameter object syntax when calling these converter functions. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-f061d089a2592ca77a52ca1f40d810d7519493bc40fca1602ca4382d3f82c7acL143-R148) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-f061d089a2592ca77a52ca1f40d810d7519493bc40fca1602ca4382d3f82c7acL159-R163) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-f061d089a2592ca77a52ca1f40d810d7519493bc40fca1602ca4382d3f82c7acL238-R242) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-f061d089a2592ca77a52ca1f40d810d7519493bc40fca1602ca4382d3f82c7acL312-R316) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-aaab02dbd48c678bfb4454fa569ba0774000b4d83b6c27ccd5fa672deb09c64eL97-R101)

title: Render axis badges with depth-aware occlusion
description: Changes the rendering of axis badges to use depth-aware occlusion by enabling depth testing and disabling depth writing.

title: Refactor CAD component workbench into modular components, hooks, and utilities
description: This pull request refactors the CAD component workbench by breaking it down into modular components, hooks, and utilities. This change aims to improve code maintainability, readability, and reusability, aligning with best practices in React development.

title: Regenerate dataset with leatest kicad-to-circuit-json version
description: This pull request updates the dataset by regenerating it using the latest version of kicad-to-circuit-json. The changes primarily involve updating the component types from simple_diode to simple_led and simple_chip to simple_fiducial across multiple JSON files in the circuit-json directory. This ensures that the dataset reflects the most current definitions and standards for the components used in the projects.

title: Add TI footprint library support for loading KiCad archives
description: Add support for loading TI footprint libraries from KiCad archives, including functions for extracting and converting footprints to Circuit JSON without writing to disk.
body:
## Summary

- add `createTiFootprintLibrary(...)` to resolve `ti:` footprints from bridge-exported KiCad archives
- add `readFirstKicadModFromArchive(...)` for in-memory `.kicad_mod` extraction
- add `createTiPlatformConfig(...)` as a convenience helper for wiring `partsEngine` and `footprintLibraryMap.ti`
- export the new TI helpers from the package entrypoint
- update the example and README with `footprint="ti:MSP430"` usage
- add tests for archive reading, footprint conversion, and `RootCircuit` integration

## Why

This prepares `@tscircuit/ti-parts-engine` for flows like `footprint="ti:MSP430"` by downloading a KiCad archive from the TI bridge, extracting the first `.kicad_mod` in memory, and converting it to Circuit JSON without writing vendor files to disk.

title: Add endpoints for the fake according to the ul bridge pilot
description: Adds new API endpoints for exporting fake parts in KiCad format and searching for parts by MPN or UID, including middleware for authorization.

title: Initialize SPICE parser library structure
description: Scaffolds spicets following the tscircuit parser-library guide with a tokenized parse pipeline, registered class-based SPICE card model, root netlistlibrary classes, parse functions, deterministic serialization, unknown-card preservation, public exports, README API docs, and focused unitround-trip fixture tests.
body: Scaffolds spicets following the tscircuit parser-library guide with a tokenized parse pipeline, registered class-based SPICE card model, root netlist/library classes, parse functions, deterministic serialization, unknown-card preservation, public exports, README API docs, and focused unit/round-trip fixture tests.

title: Add PcbCourtyardPill definition and tests
description: Adds support for a new PCB courtyard shape called Pill (PcbCourtyardPill) with schema, type definitions, documentation, and tests.
body:
This pull request adds support for a new PCB courtyard shape called "Pill" (`PcbCourtyardPill`) which is alredy present in `props` repo. It introduces the schema, type definitions, documentation, and tests for this new shape, and integrates it into the existing PCB data model and exports.

**New PCB Courtyard Shape: Pill**

* Added a new schema and type definition for `PcbCourtyardPill`, representing a pill-shaped courtyard on a PCB, including properties such as `center`, `width`, `height`, `radius`, `layer`, and optional `color` (`src/pcb/pcb_courtyard_pill.ts`).
* Updated the PCB element type union and exports to include `PcbCourtyardPill`, ensuring it is recognized as a valid PCB element throughout the codebase (`src/pcb/index.ts`, `src/any_circuit_element.ts`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-3022bee8c52a8b47d5aaf08d7d943adbf9687980d9146d2a62ab07abdaaa138cR73) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-3022bee8c52a8b47d5aaf08d7d943adbf9687980d9146d2a62ab07abdaaa138cR135) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-3022bee8c52a8b47d5aaf08d7d943adbf9687980d9146d2a62ab07abdaaa138cR198) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-e9b8dd38a2227129c7ad89d805c91b5c8a403568d20681fb621143adb580f792R122)
* Added documentation for `PcbCourtyardPill` in the `README.md`, detailing its properties and usage. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R118) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R1492-R1514)

**Testing**

* Introduced a unit test for parsing the new `pcb_courtyard_pill` schema, verifying correct parsing and property values (`tests/pcb_courtyard.test.ts`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-7f2fb52ce186846550f3c4e769bb41c040072dd39d934a47c8b926cf08456851R5) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-7f2fb52ce186846550f3c4e769bb41c040072dd39d934a47c8b926cf08456851R59-R76)

title: introduce simulation_spice_subcircuit

title: Fix findBoundsAndCenter for circular_hole_with_rect_pad plated holes
description: Fixes bounds calculation for circular_hole_with_rect_pad plated holes by correctly handling rect_pad_width, rect_pad_height, and hole_diameter properties.
body: `getDebugLayoutObject` didn't recognize `rect_pad_width`/`rect_pad_height` or `hole_diameter` properties, causing `findBoundsAndCenter` to fall back to 0.1x0.1 bounds for `circular_hole_with_rect_pad` pads. Added handling for these properties so bounds are computed correctly.

title: Add dash length and gap props for schematic line and path
description: Adds dashLength and dashGap support to schematicline  and schematicpath , matching the existing dash_length and dash_gap fields in circuit-json.
body:
## What changed

Adds `dashLength` and `dashGap` support to `<schematicline />` and `<schematicpath />`, matching the existing `dash_length` and `dash_gap` fields in `circuit-json`.

## Why

`circuit-json` already models dash length and gap for schematic lines and paths, but `@tscircuit/props` did not expose corresponding JSX props. This lets consumers configure schematic dash patterns through props instead of being limited to the default dashed behavior.

## Validation

- `bun test tests/schematic-line.test.ts tests/schematic-path.test.ts`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun scripts/generate-component-types.ts`
- `bun scripts/generate-manual-edits-docs.ts`
- `bun scripts/generate-readme-docs.ts`
- `bun scripts/generate-props-overview.ts`
- `bun run format:check lib/components/schematic-line.ts lib/components/schematic-path.ts tests/schematic-line.test.ts tests/schematic-path.test.ts`

title: Add schematic section title font size prop
description: Adds an optional sectionTitleFontSize prop to schematicsection , allowing callers to configure schematic section title sizing.
body:
## Summary

Adds an optional `sectionTitleFontSize` prop to `<schematicsection />`, allowing callers to configure schematic section title sizing.

## Changes

- Added `sectionTitleFontSize?: number | string` to `SchematicSectionProps`
- Added `sectionTitleFontSize: distance.optional()` to `schematicSectionProps`
- Regenerated component type and props documentation

## Validation

- `bun scripts/generate-component-types.ts`
- `bun scripts/generate-manual-edits-docs.ts`
- `bun scripts/generate-readme-docs.ts`
- `bun scripts/generate-props-overview.ts`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun run format:check`

description: Adds support for spicemodel in chip properties and introduces a new spicemodel component for handling SPICE simulations.

title: fix: When `<chip />` and `<smtpad />` both have layer as `bottom` then the footprint should render it on the `bottom` layer
description: Fixes rendering issue where chip  and smtpad  components with layer set to bottom were not displayed correctly on the bottom layer.

title: Respect sectionTitleFontSize for schematic section labels
description: Adds support for sectionTitleFontSize in schematic section labels, allowing customization of label font size instead of defaulting to 0.18.
body:
## What changed
- Added `sectionTitleFontSize` support to schematic section labels.
- The label now uses the provided size instead of always falling back to `0.18`.
- Added a focused test that checks the emitted schematic text font size and updated the snapshot.

## Why
- This lets callers control section title emphasis in schematic layouts without changing the default behavior for existing sections.

## Validation
- `BUN_UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 bun test tests/components/primitive-components/schematic-section.test.tsx`

title: Fix breakout point SRJ construction to route through boundary points
description: Fixes routing issues by ensuring breakout point coordinates are correctly handled for cross-boundary traces, preventing duplication of internal traces in parent SRJ.
body:
- Filter directTraceConnections by subcircuit_id so internal traces routed inside a breakout are not duplicated in the parent's SRJ
- Add breakout point coordinates as additional points in pointsToConnect for cross-boundary traces, ensuring the autorouter routes through the boundary crossing
- Skip breakout points that are already handled by cross-boundary traces; only create direct [port, bp] connections for manual breakout points without a cross-boundary trace

title: Add custom SPICE subcircuit models via <spicemodel />
description: Adds a Spicemodel component that can be passed through spiceModelspicemodel  on normal components to emit simulation_spice_subcircuit circuit-json. The implementation uses spicets to parse .subckt definitions, maps SPICE pins to component ports through spicePinMapping or matching pin names.
body:
Adds a Spicemodel component that can be passed through spiceModel={`<spicemodel />`} on normal
  components to emit simulation_spice_subcircuit circuit-json. The implementation uses spicets
  to parse .subckt definitions, maps SPICE pins to component ports through spicePinMapping or
  matching pin names.

title: fix: `orginalLayer` and` layer` prop both have it as `bottom` but due to missing layer prop on `<chip />` it was rendering on top due to flip bug
description: Fixes rendering issue where chip  components were incorrectly displayed on top layer due to missing layer prop, despite both originalLayer and layer being set to bottom.

title: Add schematicrect support to symbol generation
description: Adds schematic_rect support to the symbol generator, allowing Circuit JSON schematic rectangles to round-trip into tscircuit symbols with preserved attributes.
body:
## What changed

- Added `schematic_rect` support to the symbol generator.
- Mapped Circuit JSON `center` to tscircuit `schX` / `schY` and preserved width, height, rotation, stroke width, color, fill state, fill color, and dashed styling.
- Extended the schematic-elements fixture and updated the SVG snapshot to cover the new shape.

## Why

- This lets Circuit JSON schematic rectangles round-trip into tscircuit symbols instead of being dropped.
- The mapping follows the local docs contract for `<schematicrect />`.

## Validation

- `bun test`

title: Support pcb_copper_text in footprint conversion
description: Adds support for reading and rendering pcb_copper_text elements in footprint conversion, including attributes for positioning, alignment, font, and layer handling.
body:
## Changes

- read `pcb_copper_text` elements from circuit JSON in `generateFootprintTsx`
- emit `<coppertext />` with:
  - anchor position/alignment
  - text
  - font / fontSize
  - optional `pcbRotation`
  - optional `knockout`
  - optional `mirrored`
  - non-default layer handling
- add dedicated test coverage for copper text conversion
- add PCB SVG snapshot coverage for rendered copper text

title: document dashed outlines for schematic rectangles
description: Updates the schematicrect documentation to include dashed outlines, adds an example of a dashed rectangle, and documents the isDashed prop in the props table.
body:
## Summary
- Update the `schematicrect` docs to mention dashed outlines.
- Add a dashed rectangle example.
- Document the `isDashed` prop in the props table.

## Validation
- `bun run typecheck`

title: Add browser-safe PNG rendering for glTF scenes without Node dependencies
description: Add a browser-safe rendering path for glTF scenes that returns plain Uint8Array instead of Node Buffer, using OffscreenCanvas and createImageBitmap for encodingdecoding.
body: ### Problem `renderGLTFToPNGBuffer` and related helpers depend on Node-only APIs (`pureImageFactory`, `encodePNGToBuffer`, `decodeImageFromBuffer` via pureimage's Node entry point). These fail in browsers because pureimage's browser entry doesn't export `encodePNGToStream`, `decodePNGFromStream`, or `make()` returning a `BitmapLike` — it returns a Canvas element instead. ### Solution Add a browser-safe rendering path that returns plain `Uint8Array` instead of Node `Buffer`, using `OffscreenCanvas` + `createImageBitmap` for encoding/decoding, with dynamic imports of pureimage only on the Node path. ### Changes **New files:** - `lib/image/encodePNG.ts` — PNG encoder using `OffscreenCanvas` + `convertToBlob` in browser, dynamically imports pureimage in Node. Returns `Uint8Array`. - `lib/render/renderGLTFToPNGFromURL.ts` — URL render helper using `createUint8Bitmap` + `encodePNG`. Returns `Uint8Array`. - `lib/render/renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB.ts` — GLB render helper using `createUint8Bitmap` + `encodePNG`. Returns `Uint8Array`. **Modified files:** - `lib/index.ts` — Added exports: `encodePNG`, `renderGLTFToPNGFromURL`, `renderGLTFToPNGFromGLB` - `cli/index.ts` — Added `encodePNG` re-export - `lib/gltf/resourceUtils.ts` — `decodeImageFromBuffer` now uses `createImageBitmap` + `OffscreenCanvas` in browser, dynamically imports pureimage in Node - `site/examples/browser-compat.page.tsx` — Uses new browser-safe APIs, renders three PNG images (empty scene, soic8.gltf, arduino-uno.glb) as visual proof - `playwright/browser-compat.pw.ts` — Unskipped. Asserts: no `Buffer`/`process` globals, `Uint8Array` returns, valid PNG signatures, correct dimensions, GLB rendering in Chromium **Deleted files:** - `tests/basics/browser-first-api.test.ts` — Redundant; Playwright test covers the same assertions in a real browser ### Backward compatibility All existing APIs are untouched: - `renderGLTFToPNGBuffer` / `renderGLTFToPNGBufferFromURL` / `renderGLTFToPNGBufferFromGLBBuffer` — still return `Buffer` - `encodePNGToBuffer` — still returns `Buffer` - `pureImageFactory` — still works in Node - All 21 bun tests pass unchanged ### Architecture | Path | Bitmap | Encoder | Return type | |------|--------|---------|-------------| | **Node** | `pureImageFactory` | `encodePNGToBuffer` (pureimage streams) | `Buffer` | | **Browser** | `createUint8Bitmap` | `encodePNG` (OffscreenCanvas) | `Uint8Array` | Dynamic `import()` ensures `readable-stream` and `pureimage` are only loaded on the Node path — the browser bundle stays clean. ### Testing - `bun test` — 21 pass, 0 fail - Playwright (`bun run test:playwright`) — Chromium browser test verifies: - No `Buffer` or `process` globals before import - In-memory render returns `Uint8Array` with valid `\x89PNG` signature and correct dimensions (256×192) - URL render (`soic8.gltf`) returns `Uint8Array` with valid PNG signature - GLB render (`arduino-uno.glb`) returns `Uint8Array` with valid PNG signature

title: repro: add repro21 coverage for all PCB cutout shapes
description: Adds test coverage for various PCB cutout shapes including rectangle, circle, and polygon in the repro21 test suite.

title: Add model loading progress tracking and download status feedback
description: Adds progress tracking for model loading and provides download status feedback to users during the loading process.

title: Align CAD component placement using model geometry bounds and anchor alignment offsets
description: Aligns CAD component placement by utilizing model geometry bounds and anchor alignment offsets for improved accuracy in positioning.

title: Add fit-to-view camera framing with scene-aware bounds and board-aware viewport fitting
description: Adds functionality to fit the camera view to the scene bounds, enhancing the visualization of components in the CAD environment.

title: Add interactive 3D axis compass for camera orientation and view selection
description: Adds an interactive 3D axis compass to the SceneCanvas component for improved camera orientation and view selection.

title: Update poppygl dependency to version 0.0.23
description: Updates the poppygl dependency to version 0.0.23 in the package.json file.
body:
This pull request updates the `poppygl` dependency to a newer version in the `package.json` file.

- Dependency update:
  * Upgraded `poppygl` from version `^0.0.20` to `^0.0.23` in `package.json`.

title: add formatter and github workflows
description: This pull request introduces several GitHub workflows for formatting checks, testing, type checking, and publishing to npm. It also adds a configuration file for the Biome formatter, which standardizes code formatting across the project. The workflows are designed to run on pushes and pull requests to the main branch, ensuring that code adheres to formatting standards and passes tests before being merged.
